title: phragmites australis subsp. chrysanthus
intro: Phragmites australis subsp. chrysanthus, commonly known as golden cordgrass, is a subspecies of the phragmites australis plant, a type of perennial grass found in wetlands and marshy areas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of golden cordgrass include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Golden cordgrass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk remedies for its purported ability to promote wound healing and reduce swelling.
